Fix budget proration and wire up period remaining adjustments

Proration fix:
- Replace daily-rate proration with occurrence-based calculation.
  A biweekly \ item in a 15-day period now shows \ (1
  occurrence) instead of \ (daily rate × days).
- occurrencesInPeriod rounds to nearest whole occurrence, with
  a minimum of 1 per period.

Period remaining fix:
- Checking off a budget item now updates that period's Remaining
  balance, not just the final Free Spending total.
- Period 1 checkoffs also cascade into Period 2's starting balance
  and remaining.

commit.message:
Initial commit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite spec.org to align with corrected architecture

Major changes:
- Remove invented concepts: directed program graphs, IR, MSBuild targets
  for graph validation, three-tier binding, hot-swap AssemblyLoadContext,
  NuGet artifact packaging as core arch, CLI as core arch, 7+ diagnostics
- Add separate TInput/TOutput design: plain C# record inputs, [LmpSignature]
  partial record outputs, Predictor<TIn,TOut> binds them
- Add DSPy-grounded modules: ChainOfThought, BestOfN, Refine, ReActAgent,
  ProgramOfThought (post-MVP), BootstrapFewShot, BootstrapRandomSearch,
  MIPROv2 (post-MVP), Evaluator
- Add actual prompt format: source-gen PromptBuilder<TIn,TOut> creates
  ChatMessage[] with [[ ## field ## ]] delimiters, GetResponseAsync<TOut>
- Update platform primitives: .NET 10 / C# 14 / Aspire GA, IChatClient only
- Move ML.NET, Infer.NET, Z3 to post-MVP extensions table
- Reframe from framework to building blocks that compile
- Source gen focus: PromptBuilder, JsonTypeInfo, GetPredictors, CoT extended
  output record, 2-3 diagnostics (LMP001-LMP003)
- Keep all [1]-[11] business case citations with URLs
- Keep platform primitive map (Section 2A), updated for new architecture
- Document intentionally excluded concepts (Section 22)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Rewrite source generator spec to focus on emitted artifacts

Refocus the spec on the five things the generator actually emits:

1. PromptBuilder<TInput, TOutput> — assembles ChatMessage[] from
   instructions, input/output field metadata, demos, and current input.
   Shows the actual prompt format (system, demo pairs, current input).

2. JsonTypeInfo<TOutput> — source-generated JSON serialization context
   for zero-reflection AOT-safe serialization.

3. GetPredictors() on LmpModule — returns all Predictor<,> fields
   without runtime reflection (replaces DSPy named_predictors()).

4. ChainOfThought extended output — internal record adding Reasoning
   field to TOutput when ChainOfThought<TIn,TOut> is used.

5. Diagnostics (LMP001-LMP003 only):
   - LMP001: Output property missing [Description]
   - LMP002: Non-JSON-serializable output property
   - LMP003: [LmpSignature] on non-partial record

Dropped: graph IR, program/step/edge descriptors, binding generators,
MSBuild tasks, DI registration helpers, 7-diagnostic catalog.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Simplify artifact format spec to match DSPy save/load

Replace the over-engineered artifact format (CompiledArtifact, XxHash128,
SHA-256, AssemblyLoadContext hot-swap, NuGet packaging, polymorphic
StepParameter types, ZIP archives) with a flat JSON format that mirrors
DSPy's module.save()/module.load() pattern.

New format: {version, module, predictors: {name: {instructions, demos,
config}}} — one entry per predictor with instructions, few-shot demos,
and optional LM config. SaveAsync/LoadAsync live directly on LmpModule.

Dropped: variantId, baseProgramHash, validationMetrics, approved,
provenance, bindingMetadata, ICompiledArtifactLoader interface,
FileSystemArtifactLoader, HotSwapArtifactProvider,
CollectibleArtifactContext, ArtifactHashing, .lmpa bundle format.

Post-MVP extensions (NuGet packaging, version hashing, hot reload,
metrics sidecar) are noted briefly but not designed in detail.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: set init_params='stc' to fully bypass KMeans in GaussianHMM

Pre-setting model.means_ alone is insufficient -- hmmlearn still runs
KMeans when 'm' is in init_params (default 'stmc').  Exclude 'm' so
hmmlearn's _needs_init check returns False and our percentile-based
means are respected without the KMeans code path executing at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix ticker schedule accuracy and add day-duration overflow guard (#4)

Two correctness issues in the recurring sync interval logic: the "next
run" timestamp was anchored to the wrong point in time, and day-based
durations could silently overflow `int64`, causing a panic in
`time.NewTicker`.

## Changes

**`cmd/collection-sync/main.go`**
- Move `time.NewTicker` to before the initial sync so the schedule is
anchored to process start, not sync completion — the first "next run"
message was previously off by the sync duration
- Replace `time.Now().Add(interval)` inside the loop with
`tick.Add(interval)` — `tick` is the ticker's scheduled fire time, so
this correctly reflects the next tick even when sync duration exceeds
the interval

**`internal/config/duration.go`**
- Guard against `time.Duration` overflow when converting large day
values; return an explicit error instead of producing a negative
duration that panics the ticker

```go
maxDays := int64(time.Duration(1<<63-1) / (24 * time.Hour))
if int64(days) > maxDays {
    return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid interval %q: exceeds maximum supported duration", s)
}
```

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nipuna-perera <11575062+nipuna-perera@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: rewrite system-architecture.md from first principles

Replace the over-engineered architecture (IR layers, program graphs,
MSBuild targets, three-tier binding) with a simpler 4-layer design:

- Layer 0: .NET Platform (IChatClient, M.E.AI — zero LMP code)
- Layer 1: Core Building Blocks (Predictor<TIn,TOut>, LmpModule, source gen)
- Layer 2: Reasoning Strategies (ChainOfThought, BestOfN, ReAct — thin wrappers)
- Layer 3: Optimization (BootstrapFewShot, Evaluator, IOptimizer)

Key changes: separate TInput/TOutput types, source generators as the
primary .NET advantage, building blocks not a framework.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lmp-dotnet
repository.description: LMP (Language Model Programs) — A .NET 10/C# 14 framework for authoring, optimizing, and deploying language model programs. Inspired by DSPy, native to .NET.

commit.message:
Add TARGET_APPLE to System.Private.CoreLib DefineConstants for Apple platforms (#126652)

`TypeLoadException: Could not load type
'System.Runtime.InteropServices.Swift.SwiftSelf' from assembly
'System.Private.CoreLib'` on tvos_arm64 CoreCLR. The Swift interop types
(`SwiftSelf`, `SwiftError`, `SwiftIndirectResult`) were being trimmed
from `System.Private.CoreLib.dll` by ILLink on Apple builds.

The `CreateRuntimeRootILLinkDescriptorFile` task generates the ILLink
root descriptor by processing `corelib.h` with
`DefineConstants="$(DefineConstants);FOR_ILLINK"`. Since `corelib.h`
gates the Swift type definitions behind `#ifdef TARGET_APPLE`, and
`TARGET_APPLE` was absent from the C# `DefineConstants`, the task didn't
emit preservation roots for these types. ILLink then trimmed them as
unreachable, causing `CoreLibBinder::GetClass(CLASS__SWIFT_SELF)` to
throw at runtime.

Fixes https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/126557

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jkotas <6668460+jkotas@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Jan Kotas <jkotas@microsoft.com>

repository.name: runtime
repository.description: .NET is a cross-platform runtime for cloud, mobile, desktop, and IoT apps.

commit.message:
Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

* Fix SharpMUSH.Generated.generated.sln to reference correct project file

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/7c0166d6-0167-4ddb-b3c4-a81a17b82acc

Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore race in MemgraphDatabase async enumerables

Add FreshEnumerable<T> wrapper that calls the factory on every GetAsyncEnumerator()
call, ensuring each enumeration gets a brand-new async state machine instead of
cloning the original cached one.

Previously, async IAsyncEnumerable<T> state machines were created once and cached
inside Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<T>>. The C# runtime's GetAsyncEnumerator() clone
mechanism reuses internal fields (including ManualResetValueTaskSourceCore) in ways
that produce a race when the same IAsyncEnumerable is enumerated concurrently -
particularly via the .GetAwaiter().GetResult() path in SharpMUSHBooleanExpressionVisitor's
lock-expression trees, which blocks thread pool threads and amplifies contention.

Fixes all Lazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> and AsyncLazy<IAsyncEnumerable<>> factories in
MapNodeToSharpObject, MapToSharpAttribute, and MapToLazySharpAttribute.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/SharpMUSH/SharpMUSH/sessions/023e7ce9-f225-4c2e-8d50-a902d9125d42

Co-authored-by: HarryCordewener <5649138+HarryCordewener@users.noreply.github.com>

regime,patterns: bypass KMeans init in clustering and Elliott GMM

Same worker-thread hang fix as the GaussianHMM commit: sklearn KMeans
blocks indefinitely in asyncio.to_thread on Windows when joblib probes
CPU topology.

- regime clustering: pre-compute centroids from evenly-spaced rows so
  KMeans++ is skipped; add sample-count guard for k > n edge case.
- Elliott wave GMM: use init_params='random_from_data' to avoid the
  internal KMeans seed step.

Fix scope-based extension resource parameter ordering and ValidateResourceId generation (#57934)

1. Generator (OperationContext.cs): Fix stack push order for variable-key/variable-value
   pairs in BuildContextualParameterHierarchy. The LIFO stack was producing reversed
   positional order, causing Id.Name and Id.ResourceType.Type to be swapped in REST
   call arguments for extension resources like ChaosTargets and KubernetesClusterExtensions.

2. Emitter (resource-metadata.ts): Fix getExpectedParentResourceType to handle complex
   parent paths with more than 3 segments. For resources with nested multi-provider
   scopes (e.g., managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...),
   falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the last /providers/
   segment, enabling ValidateResourceId generation for these collections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Two-period spending money with 3-day bill lead time

Overhaul useSpendingMoney to look across two pay periods:
- Period 1: checking balance minus bills due before next paycheck
- + Paycheck incoming
- Period 2: minus bills due before second paycheck
- Budget items prorated across the full window
- = Free Spending

Bills use a 3-day lead time for period slotting — a bill due on
the 1st is effectively slotted into the EOM pay period (due date
minus 3 days = 28th/29th). This ensures the mortgage and similar
bills are attributed to the paycheck that actually needs to cover
them, not the one after the due date.

Update SpendingMoneyBreakdown to show both periods separately
with individual bill line items, the incoming paycheck, and the
budget waterfall.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
forecast: bypass KMeans init in GaussianHMM to fix MCP worker-thread hang

hmmlearn.GaussianHMM._init() runs sklearn KMeans to seed means, which
blocks indefinitely in asyncio.to_thread worker threads on Windows when
joblib probes CPU topology.  Pre-set percentile-based means so hmmlearn
skips the KMeans step -- identical pattern to the existing
_gaussian_mixture_init_kwargs workaround for GaussianMixture.

Fixes forecast_barrier_optimize timing out after 120s via MCP while
completing in ~3s via CLI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: pre-fetch UTXO address before write lock to prevent spawn_blocking stall

Both the anchor check and old_addr_to_remove squatter-eviction check were calling
utxo_manager.get_utxo().await (which uses spawn_blocking internally) while holding
masternodes.write().await.  This caused tasks to queue on the write lock and starved
tokio workers, producing the RPC-timeout / watchdog-restart cycle seen on live nodes.

Fix: look up the UTXO address once before taking the write lock (prefetched_utxo_addr),
then reuse that value inside the lock for both checks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(api): exhaustive code audit with patterns & algorithms

PATRONES Y ALGORITMOS:
- Extract Methods: executeProvisioningSteps() reduce 63→32 lines
- Functional Programming: reduce() en compose-generator, stripe/index
- Immutability: let→const en 7 archivos (-54% mutables)
- Safe Operation: error-handling.ts helpers (safeOp, retry, parallel)
- Early Return: docker/container scope reduction
- Exponential Backoff: retryWithBackoff() para operaciones flaky

RESULTADOS:
✅ Complejidad ciclomática: 43→25 (-38%)
✅ orchestrator.ts: 63 líneas → 32 líneas en main loop
✅ error-handling: 4 utilidades reutilizables
✅ 155 tests passing (100%)
✅ TypeScript: 11/11 successful
✅ ESLint: 0 warnings (complexity <10, lines-per-function <50)

Patrón: Reducer, Immutability, Extract Method, Early Return, Safe Operations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add knowledge capture foundation 1.3.0 (#21)

## Summary
- add the new `knowledge-compound` workflow skill and shared knowledge
artifact template
- wire optional prior-learning reuse into `planning-orchestration` and
`diff-review-orchestration`
- update workflow docs, manifests, marketplace metadata, changelog, and
the durable batch report for the 1.3.0 release

## Validation
- `npm --prefix plugins/workflow-orchestration test`
- `npm --prefix plugins/workflow-orchestration run validate:runtime`
- `npm run validate:plugin`
- `npm test`
- final combined gate: `npm test && npm run validate:plugin && npm
--prefix plugins/workflow-orchestration test && npm --prefix
plugins/workflow-orchestration run validate:runtime`

## Workflow outcome
- discovery-reuse: yes
- rescue-attempts: 1
- abandonment-events: 0
- final-gate-result: ready

## Notes
- durable batch summary:
`docs/knowledge-capture-foundation-1-3-batch-report.md`
- `.sdd/` was kept out of the branch and PR as requested.

---------

commit.message:
feat: backup import/upload + dev docker-compose (#12)

* feat: add backup import/upload and dev docker-compose

Add ability to upload .tar.gz backup archives via the UI and register
them as first-class BackupRecord entries (ADR 0025). Imported backups
support restore, diff, download, pin, and label like any other backup.

- New import service with defense-in-depth validation (size limits,
  whitelist, symlink/path traversal rejection, JSON array check)
- API endpoint POST /api/instance/<slug>/import/ (multipart/form-data)
- New "import" trigger type on BackupRecord
- Import modal on instance dashboard with file, label, and notes inputs
- IMPORT_SUCCESS/IMPORT_FAILED notification events
- 24 new tests (244 total, all passing)

Also:
- Update docker-compose.yml for dev use (port 9473, no volumes, no
  container_name) so it can run alongside production (port 9472)
- Update CLAUDE.md to use repo compose for dev commands
- ADR housekeeping: mark 0023 as Implemented, update 0001 todos and
  future feature ideas with completed items

* fix: resolve ruff lint errors

- B904: chain exceptions with `raise ... from exc`
- I001: sort imports in api.py
- E501: wrap long lines in tests and api.py
- SIM115: context manager already used (ruff false positive resolved by chaining)

* fix: resolve SIM115 lint — use tarfile.open as context manager

* Update docker-compose.yml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply ruff format to pass CI lint check

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #12

- Add import_failed notifications on validation/error paths in API view
- Add migration for TRIGGER_CHOICES adding "import" value
- Use Path.is_relative_to() instead of string startswith for path containment
- Catch broad exceptions in parse_flows after archive is written to disk
- Validate label length (400 → ImportValidationError) instead of silent truncation
- Bound archive member count and reject duplicates in validation
- Check TarInfo.size before reading to prevent decompression bombs
- Update ADR 0025 status to Implemented

* fix: address code review findings on PR #12

- Fix duplicate step comments (two #5) in import validation
- Remove redundant flows.json size check (already bounded by per-member check)
- Remove dead success=False branch from _notify_import
- Add missing test for label length validation
- Fix E501 lint errors in import_service and migration
- Add IMPORT_MAX_SIZE to README env vars table
- Add import events to README notification events list
- Mark ADR 0025 todos as completed
- Add clarifying comment on DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #12

- Add import-failed notification for oversized upload early return
- Handle non-JSON error responses in import fetch to show actual errors
- Add tests for import notification and retention side effects

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #12

- Add 64 KB buffer to DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE for multipart overhead
- Add IMPORT_SUCCESS/IMPORT_FAILED to Discord color map
- Set filename field on import-failed notification payload

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #12

- Increase DATA_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE headroom to 10MB so oversized
  uploads reach the view for proper JSON 413 + notification handling
- Catch json.JSONDecodeError/TypeError in _compute_changes() so a
  corrupted previous backup doesn't cause a 500 on valid imports

* fix: address Copilot review feedback on PR #12

Normalize filename to lowercase for extension check and update error
message to mention both .tar.gz and .tgz extensions.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
